buddy.name: Buddy
buddy.idle_alt: Tap me. You can pet me too.

home.idle_calm: It's okay. I'm here with you.
home.morning_nudge_sub: A small start helps the day feel steadier.
home.morning_nudge_speak: Morning check-in. Let's pick a mission.

beforeReward.unlocked_speak: Nice work. Your reward is ready.

onboarding.title: Hi! What is your child's name?
onboarding.meet_after_pet_sub: I can stay with you while you earn little stars.
onboarding.tiny_fact_bear_sleep: Did you know bears can sleep through the winter?
onboarding.age_sub: You can skip this for now.

parent_onboarding.screen1_sub: RealoKids helps children build daily habits with calm guidance, simple choices, and rewards set by parents.
parent_onboarding.screen1_note: Designed by a father for his own neurodivergent child — simple enough for any family.
parent_onboarding.screen2_body: Sit with your child the first time. Pet Buddy together, then let your child choose the first mission.
parent_onboarding.screen2_note: A few calm minutes together at the start are usually enough.
parent_onboarding.screen3_body: Before handing the app to your child, open settings and adjust the real-life pieces. Turn parts of the app on or off. You can always fine-tune it later.
parent_onboarding.screen3_note: The defaults are ready to use. A few parent tweaks can make the app feel familiar right away.
parent_onboarding.screen4_body: Slow rhythmic breathing can help the body move from stress toward calm. This is physiology, not just a metaphor.
parent_onboarding.screen4_handpan: Original handpan music can play quietly to support breathing.
parent_onboarding.screen4_try: Try it now — follow the circle
parent_onboarding.screen4_science: The longer exhale is the key signal that tells the nervous system to slow down. This pattern was adapted and tested with a real child.

demo.intro_sub: Three simple tasks to warm up.
demo.complete_msg: Ready to try a real mission?

missionPick.title: Pick a mission
missionPick.before_reward_hint: A small step now, your reward after.
missionPick.before_reward_empty_sub: There are no small missions waiting right now.
missionPick.before_reward_empty_hint: Go back and choose your reward.
missionPick.first_sub: Pick one. Buddy will stay with you.
missionPick.first_hint: Tap a card to begin. Hold it to hear it first.
missionPick.encore_sub: Tomorrow Buddy will bring a fresh set.
missionPick.hint: Hold a card to hear it

celebrate.title: Mission done! 🎉
celebrate.preference_love_speak: Nice work. Maybe later there is time for something like {{love}}.

morning.ready_speak: Great. You are ready for the day.
morning.complete_speak: Great. Morning started. You are ready for the day.

breathing.title: Breathe together
breathing.subtitle: Two minutes of calm breathing with Buddy
breathing.pet_hint: Watch my breath and try to pet me when I exhale if you want.
breathing.comfort_prompt: If it helps, think of {{comfort}} for a moment.

progress.label_lt5: You are starting. That is great.

milestone.twenty: Twenty stars. You grow every day.
milestone.hundred: One hundred stars. A real star!

7.title: Make a strong fist for three seconds
7.subtitle: Then release

11.title: Ask mom or dad how to help
11.subtitle: A small task
11.subtitle_teen: Ask or notice what is needed

15.title: Reach up high
15.subtitle: Hands as high as you can

dailySuggestions.10: Reach up. As high as you can.

settings.title: Parent settings
settings.snapshot_privacy: Stored only on this device. Easy to screenshot for parent feedback.
settings.progress_empty: No missions completed yet. Stats will appear here.
settings.child_handoff_title: Ready to give the app to your child?
settings.child_handoff_body: Adjust routines, rewards and settings if needed. When you're ready, hand over the app and start Buddy's first hello.
settings.child_handoff_step_adjust: Adjust routines, rewards and settings if needed
settings.child_start_title: Start your child's first hello with Buddy
settings.child_start_sub: After you adjust routines and rewards, hand over the app and let Buddy say hello.
settings.pin_label: PIN for rewards and Parent zone
settings.pin_sublabel: Protect rewards and Parent zone access (recommended)
settings.pin_mission_sublabel: Require parent PIN before stars are added
settings.pin_change_sublabel: PIN is set for rewards and Parent zone
settings.pin_delete_msg: Reward protection will be turned off
settings.control_hands_on_hint_body: Parent stays closely involved. Rewards require parent confirmation, and you can also lock mission completion with the parent PIN.
settings.control_standard_hint_body: Buddy keeps the child flow mostly independent. Rewards and Parent zone can still use the parent PIN. Mission completion asks for a PIN only if you turn on Lock mission completion with PIN.
settings.control_independent_hint_body: Child can move through missions and rewards with fewer parent interruptions. Parent zone remains separate, and PIN protection follows the security switches above.
settings.missions_parent_hint: Manage missions in the Parent zone
settings.infinity_loop_sub: A small set of missions each day, refreshed in the morning
settings.missions_per_day_sub: How many missions appear on the pick screen
settings.bonus_mission_sub: Offer one extra when all are done
settings.rotation_soon_sub: Use this only if you prefer a smaller rotating set instead of the daily mission set
settings.buddy_dj_sub: Some kids enjoy playful quick voice reactions on day cards. Important instructions stay clear.
settings.doodle_bg_sub: After 20 stars, show a soft background in child screens.
settings.nudge_sub: Show a gentle suggestion card on the home screen
settings.skip_sub: Buddy reacts after {{count}} skips in a row
settings.before_reward_sub_one: Use 1 small mission as a calm bridge before videos, games, snacks, stories, or parent play.
settings.before_reward_sub_many: Use {{count}} small missions as a calm bridge before videos, games, snacks, stories, or parent play.
settings.before_reward_count_sub: Start with 1. Use 2–3 only when it still feels calm.
settings.before_reward_customize_sub: Optional: choose missions and rewards for this mode.
settings.energy_sub: Buddy suggests more calm or more active missions
settings.rtl_sex_sub: Helps Buddy choose the right wording in gendered speech lines.
settings.facts_soon_sub: Buddy occasionally shares short facts and jokes during missions
settings.facts_hint_body: Facts appear during an active mission screen after a mission is picked.
settings.preferences_intro: Private notes about what your child loves and what helps them feel calm. Buddy uses them rarely, and only where they fit.
settings.preferences_loves_empty: Add motivating things, activities, or topics.
settings.preferences_comforts_empty: Add calming things, places, or routines.
settings.preferences_limit_body: Keep up to {{count}} carefully chosen items.
settings.reset_progress_sub: Stars, missions and rewards will be cleared
settings.morning_sub: Shown on the first open before noon
settings.schedule_now_sub: Show what is happening now and next on the home screen
settings.schedule_home_card_sub: Show what is happening now and what comes next on the home screen
settings.morning_reminder_sub: Show a gentle morning reminder about missions
settings.feedback_body: RealoKids is still growing. Tell us what would make it work better for your family.
settings.feedback_button: Tell us what RealoKids should improve next
settings.feedback_email_body: Hi, I wanted to share this about RealoKids:
settings.about_realo_sub: Open the parent guide again: how RealoKids works, how to start together, and why breathing helps.
settings.credits_body: Built by a father for his own neurodivergent child, and designed to be simple for any family. Made with care.
settings.reset_full_msg: Stars, missions and history will be deleted. Settings are kept.
settings.mission_delete_msg: This mission will be deleted permanently
settings.reward_delete_msg: This reward will be deleted permanently
settings.image_guardrail: Use a clear, calm square image. Emoji stays as backup.
settings.image_permission_msg: Allow photo access to add a picture for this item.

tiny_facts.f1: Your body has over 600 muscles. Standing on one leg uses most of them.
tiny_facts.f2: Touching your toes stretches your spine. It grows a tiny bit every night.
tiny_facts.f3: Jumping sends your brain a wake-up signal.
tiny_facts.f4: Your brain is 75% water. A glass of water helps it think faster.
tiny_facts.f5: One minute without a screen gives your eyes a real rest.
tiny_facts.f7: Squeezing your fists for three seconds tells your body to calm down.
tiny_facts.f8: Two minutes away from a screen helps your brain remember things better.
tiny_facts.f9: Tidying one corner makes the whole room feel different. Really.
tiny_facts.f10: A hug releases a chemical called oxytocin. It makes both people feel safer.
tiny_facts.f11: Asking for help is one of the bravest things a person can do.
tiny_facts.f12: A tidy space helps a calm mind. Even a small tidy counts.
tiny_facts.f13: Shaking your hands for ten seconds wakes up the nerves in your fingers.
tiny_facts.f14: Walking slowly while feeling the floor helps your body know where it is.
tiny_facts.f15: Stretching tall makes you breathe deeper without even trying.
tiny_facts.f16: Cold water on your face sends a signal to your heart to slow down.
tiny_facts.f17: Three slow breaths can change how your whole body feels in under a minute.
tiny_facts.f18: Drinking water more than once a day helps your brain stay ready.
tiny_facts.f19: A made bed can be the first win of the day.
tiny_facts.f20: Clearing your plate is a small act of care for the people around you.
tiny_facts.f21: Folded clothes are easier to find later.
tiny_facts.f22: Saying thank you makes the other person feel seen. That matters a lot.
tiny_facts.f23: A smile uses fewer muscles than a frown. And it's contagious.
tiny_facts.f24: Sharing something good before bed helps your brain remember the day kindly.
tiny_facts.f25: One minute of silence gives your brain a chance to sort itself out.
tiny_facts.f26: Drawing activates a different part of your brain than words do.
tiny_facts.f27: Looking out the window rests your eyes and your thoughts at the same time.
tiny_facts.f28: A few simple moves wake up your muscles and help your brain feel ready.
tiny_facts.breathing_nature: Next time you are outside, try putting your attention on your breath for just a moment. Nature makes it easier.

tiny_jokes.j1: Why did the little bear sit in the shade? He didn't want to become a gummy bear.
tiny_jokes.j2: What do bears put on their toast? Honey — always honey.
tiny_jokes.j3: What is a bear's favorite kind of story? One with a pawsitive ending.
tiny_jokes.j4: Why was the teddy bear never hungry? He was already stuffed.
tiny_jokes.j5: What do you call a bear who loves quiet time? A hiber-napper.
tiny_jokes.j6: Why did the bear bring a backpack? For his beary important things.
tiny_jokes.j7: What do bears do when they are happy? They give big bear hugs.
tiny_jokes.j8: Why did the bear do so well in school? He knew all the bear-sics.
tiny_jokes.j9: What do you call a wet bear? A drizzly bear.
tiny_jokes.j10: Why do bears make terrible secret keepers? Everything comes out in the growl.
tiny_jokes.j11: What did the bear say after a long nap? That was un-bear-lievably good.
tiny_jokes.j12: Why did the bear bring a pencil to the forest? In case he wanted to draw a crowd.
tiny_jokes.j13: What do bears wear in the rain? Drizzly bears need drizzly gear.
tiny_jokes.j14: Why did the bear stop telling jokes? Everyone said they were un-bear-able.
tiny_jokes.j15: What is a bear's favourite instrument? The growl-itar.
tiny_jokes.j16: Why did the bear sit in the library? He was looking for bear-y good books.
tiny_jokes.j17: What do you call a bear with no teeth? A gummy bear.
tiny_jokes.j18: Why did the bear win the talent show? His act was im-paw-sible to beat.
tiny_jokes.j19: What do bears do before a big day? They paws and take a breath.
tiny_jokes.j20: Why did the small bear bring a blanket to school? For show and bear.
